ArchitecturalLevels.description: A deliberately busy example that documents layers, matcher rules, allowed and forbidden type policies, site filters, wildcard rules and unrelated dependency chains.

Include.description: Imports the primary application chain so this file can focus on crosscutting, forbidden and billing-specific rules.

Forbidden.description: Names and namespaces that should never become dependencies in the documented architecture.

Class.description: A Store name hides persistence behind a retail metaphor; Repository is the supported persistence term.

Fix.description: The code fix replaces the forbidden Store suffix with Repository.

Namespace.comment: Do not depend on legacy internals.
Namespace.description: Legacy internals may exist during migration, but layered code should not take dependencies on them.

Exceptions.description: The migration adapter is the sanctioned bridge while old code is being removed.

Class.description: LegacyAdapter is the one named exception to the legacy namespace ban.

Layer.name: Crosscutting
Layer.description: Infrastructure-shaped helpers that can be requested by any layer without changing business direction.

Class.description: Logging is intentionally available across the architecture.
Telemetry is matched by full name so refactors do not accidentally broaden the layer.

Layer.name: Diagnostics
Layer.description: Diagnostics code can inspect the application, but it should not retain long-lived references.

Class.description: Diagnostics classes are operational inspection tools.

Layer.name: BillingEntry
Layer.description: A separate billing entry chain, unrelated to the primary order-processing flow.

Namespace.description: Entry points for billing live under this namespace.

Class.description: EntryPoint classes are the public billing entry surface.

Layer.name: BillingWorkflow
Layer.description: Billing use-case orchestration that sits behind billing entry points.

Allowed.description: Billing workflows use the approved Invoice naming vocabulary.

Class.description: Invoice is the approved workflow subject in this bounded example.
Gateway classes wrap external payment systems.

Forbidden.description: Unsafe gateway variants must not become dependencies.

Class.description: Unsafe integrations are explicitly blocked inside the billing gateway layer.

Layer.name: ExternalAdapters
Layer.description: Types supplied by a separately compiled adapters assembly.

Assembly.description: Assembly matching classifies referenced types without relying on namespace conventions.

AllowedDependency.description: Any layer may use crosscutting helpers, but only as short-lived collaborators.
Diagnostics may inspect configured layers, but block lists prevent retained state.
Billing entry points may call the workflow layer.
Billing workflows may call the payment gateway layer.

BlockedDependency.description: Presentation types must never bypass application services and access persistence directly.
